questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to declare a variable of type integer in Java?
questions.explanation: Option D attempts to assign a String value to an integer variable.  Java's type system prevents this direct assignment.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `main` method in a Java program?
options: B) To define methods within the class
C) To serve as the entry point for program execution
questions.explanation: The `main` method is where the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) begins executing a Java program.

questions.question: What will the following code snippet print? `System.out.println(10 + 5 + "Hello");`
questions.explanation: Java performs the addition first (10 + 5 = 15) before string concatenation.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to prevent a variable from being changed after initialization?
questions.explanation: `final` keyword declares a constant variable in Java.

questions.question: Which access modifier provides the most restrictive access to a class member?
questions.explanation: `private` members are only accessible within the same class.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `import` statement in Java?
options: C) To access classes from other packages
questions.explanation: `import` statements allow you to use classes defined in other parts of the Java class library or your own projects.

questions.question: What data type would be most appropriate to store the number of students in a class?
questions.explanation: The number of students is a whole number, making `int` the most suitable data type.

questions.question: In the code `// Hello.java`, what does `//` indicate?
options: A) The start of a multi-line comment
B) The start of a single-line comment
C) The end of a statement
questions.explanation: `//` denotes a single-line comment in Java code.  The compiler ignores text after `//` on the same line.
